name: Single Source of Truth
evidence: governance test: 'document store split stays modular'; global_state_corruption: setOwner/getOwner round-trip; DS.state is single source asserted in engine_contracts

Each subsystem with observable state has a dedicated *State.js file: ClipboardState (clipboard contents), DragState (drag session), GuideState (active guides), HistoryState (undo/redo stacks), SelectionState (selection facade over DS), SnapState (grid/snap config), RenderSchedulerState (frame/storm/hotspot state), RuntimeServicesState (owner registry)
KeyboardEngine delegates handler registry to KeyboardRegistry.js (existing SSOT) — no duplicate _handlers map

subsystem_ssot_guard: SSOT-EXIST-001 (State files exist), SSOT-STATE-001/002 (no bare let in engines), SSOT-REGISTRY-001 (KeyboardEngine uses KeyboardRegistry), SSOT-API-001 (.state/.registry exposed)
debuggability.test.mjs: 4 SSOT tests — ClipboardState isolation/mutation, DragState lifecycle, KeyboardRegistry routing, guard runner

name: Reset limpio
evidence: reset_stability_guard.mjs: exits 1 if any engine registers setInterval without storing the handle — zombie timers prevent clean soft-reset
reset_stability_guard.mjs: exits 1 if DocumentState.js adds a reset/reinit fn that skips elementCounter — partial reset causes ID collisions
RuntimeGlobals.js + RuntimeHelpers.js: initClock stores setInterval handle in window._clockInterval so it can be cleared on teardown
Confirmed: no soft-reset API exists; page reload is the only canonical reset — guard enforces this stays non-degradable
gap: Soft-reset (without page reload) is architecturally unsupported — elementCounter is module-level and non-resettable. Guard enforces the known worst-case patterns (zombie timers, partial counter resets) are absent.

evidence: Debug overlays fully encapsulated via Shadow DOM; global cascade cannot reach rf-debug-* selectors; internal queries use shadow.querySelector/getElementById; host elements remain in regular DOM for positioning/dragging

name: Métricas por fase
evidence: RenderSchedulerFrame._flush() uses performance.now() to compute durationMs per phase (layout/visual/handles/post) and total flush durationMs. frameMeta.phases[] carries {name,tasks,durationMs,slowestMs,slowestKey}. Tested via node vm sandbox: 4 phase entries, all durationMs >= 0.

name: Timeline de ejecución
evidence: frameMeta.phases[] is an ordered array built by pushing entries in priority loop order (layout→visual→handles→post). Timeline ordering asserted in test: names must equal [layout,visual,handles,post]. completeFrame event emitted with full timeline.

name: Profiling por operación
evidence: Per-task profiling: slowestMs and slowestKey per phase (PROF-SLOWEST-001). taskT0/taskMs performance.now() per task inside loop (PROF-TASK-001). Cross-frame hotspot ring: hotspots[] in RenderSchedulerState (cap 100, threshold 16ms), tasks exceeding threshold pushed with {frame,phase,key,ms} (PROF-HOTSPOT-001). getHotspots()/clearHotspots() exported (PROF-HOTSPOT-API-001). per_task_profiling_guard: 9 static checks. debuggability.test.mjs: 4 new tests (hotspot recorded, ring capped at 100, clearHotspots resets, guard runner).

name: Contador de renders
evidence: S.recentFrameTimes[] rolling ring (max 60, 1s window). On each _flush: push timestamp, count frames in window. If count > S.stormThreshold (30): log error, dispatch rf:render-storm CustomEvent, escalate to EngineCore.runtime.enterSafeMode. stormActive latches until clearStorm(). Test verifies storm fires when threshold crossed.

name: Tracking de writers
evidence: RuntimeServicesState.setOwner and RuntimeServices.setOwner both track prior owner; re-claim with same value is idempotent (boot-safe); conflict appended to _writerConflicts log (cap 50); getWriterConflicts() returns append-only snapshot. 3 runtime tests pass in node vm sandbox.

name: Writer Conflict Detection
evidence: On setOwner conflict: (1) console.error logged, (2) RF.EngineCore.runtime.enterSafeMode called with reason=writer_conflict + error + meta, (3) rf:writer-conflict CustomEvent dispatched on window. Static guard (WRITER-OWNER-001) verifies sections-layer/handles-layer/preview-content only written by SectionEngine/SelectionOverlay/PreviewEngineRenderer respectively. WRITER-CLAIM-001 verifies setOwner only called from boot-sequence files with canonical owner values.

name: Snapshot visual contractual
evidence: snapshotElements() returns [{id,sectionId,type,x,y,w,h}] — full schema tested in node vm sandbox. snapshotSections() returns [{id,stype,height}]. evidence: shared_core_guard.mjs: SHARED-DOC-001 verifies Shared Core Standards section present in testing-canon.md; SHARED-SUITES-001 verifies all four canonical test suite files exist and are referenced; SHARED-VALIDATE-001 verifies validate_repo.sh exists; SHARED-GUARDS-001 verifies audit/ has ≥5 guard files; SHARED-ENFORCEMENT-001 verifies guard referenced in Enforcement section

name: Cause over Cosmetic Fixes
evidence: cosmetic_fix_guard.mjs: exits 1 if a fix: commit touches only HTML/CSS/presentation files with no logic or test change; wired into validate_repo.sh; correctly detects commit 99bdd08 (HTML-only fix) as cosmetic, passes 30e40d5 (logic fix) and ddc8944 (multi-engine fix) as causal
gap: fix commits that change one logic line + many CSS lines pass (logic present = causal); very small logic changes may escape detection

name: Evidence-driven Debugging
evidence: debuggability: 10 tests enforce that runtime state is always JSON-serializable, has deterministic incidentKey, has DOM ownership evidence, has writer conflict log — evidence must be machine-readable

name: Three-attempt Convergence
evidence: convergence_discipline_guard.mjs: CONV-DOC-001 verifies three-attempt rule and two-file constraint present in testing-canon.md; CONV-GAP-001 verifies all documented-but-not-hard-enforced principles have a gap description (no silent undocumented debt); CONV-ENFORCEMENT-001 verifies testing-canon.md references the guard itself so the rule cannot be silently removed; wired into debuggability.test.mjs

name: Every Pain Becomes a Guardrail
evidence: pain_becomes_guardrail_guard.mjs: exits 1 if a fix: commit includes no test file change and no [guardrail: ref] in commit body; wired into validate_repo.sh; passes trivially on non-fix commits; accepts [fix-trivial] exemption for path/config fixes
gap: historical fix commits (ddc8944, 4c8949a, etc.) predate this guard and carry no test — they would all fail if re-evaluated; developers must use [guardrail: existing-test] for fixes where coverage already exists in the repo
